title: Measurement, SI Units, Dimensional Analysis, and Uncertainty · Mastery Check
summary: Measure a room, select materials, convert specifications, and report tolerances for a community repair. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a unit-safe materials estimate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose appropriate SI base and derived units and write their symbols correctly.
Convert quantities with unit factors that equal one and cancel dimensions explicitly.
Square or cube conversion factors when converting area and volume.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 8 m by 6 m. Calculate its area.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 8 m × 6 m.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 8 m × 6 m.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes m² or m^2 or square meters.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Unit Validation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.
